privacy_boundary: public repository files and commit metadata only; no private chat, health, pet-health, credential, contact, or unreviewed upload content

records.title: Aggregate queue snapshot omits later append-only team updates
records.summary: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is timestamped 2026-07-11T16:51:00-04:00 and contains R-001 through R-004, while a later append-only R-005 record marks an additional Frontier item completed at 18:19 and a later V-001 record changes the current verification commit and blocker at 18:21. These files are not mutually false; they describe different repository times. Consumers that read only the aggregate queue will reconstruct stale state.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Re-read the three exact blobs. Falsify the record if ACTIVE_QUEUE already contains R-005 and the later V-001 slice, or if either append-only update predates the aggregate snapshot.

records.title: Queue truth is a fold over aggregate snapshot plus later owned deltas
records.summary: Until an atomic reconciler is implemented, readers should treat ACTIVE_QUEUE as a base snapshot and fold later operations/queue-updates records by item owner and updated_at. A delta may extend an existing item or introduce a later item; it must not erase another team's state. This preserves the coordination rule and prevents stale aggregate data from being mistaken for current truth.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Build a deterministic fold test over the base queue and known deltas. Reject this proposal if it produces owner collisions, timestamp ambiguity, or a state different from a manually reviewed reconstruction.

records.title: No committed deterministic queue reconciler is indexed
records.summary: The repository currently exposes a base queue and append-only deltas, but this run found no indexed artifact proving a deterministic merge order, duplicate-item rule, owner-authority rule, or conflict report. The proposed fold remains a design decision until implemented and tested.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Resolve by committing a reconciler plus tests covering later timestamps, equal timestamps, duplicate item IDs, cross-owner writes, malformed deltas, and preservation of untouched team records.

reconciliation_rule.ordering: updated_at ascending, then repository commit order
reconciliation_rule.authority: only the declared owner may mutate an existing item; new item IDs require an owner
reconciliation_rule.conflict_behavior: retain both records and emit an unresolved contradiction; never silently overwrite
